fields.name: 16. Time management in debate
fields.content-01:
1. Introduction (10 minutes)

‍

  • Welcome participants and introduce the topic.
  • Explain why time management is crucial in debates, both during preparation and speeches.
  • Share the session objectives and expected outcomes.

‍

2. Discussion: Common Challenges (15 minutes)

‍

  • Facilitate a group discussion on common time management challenges in debates.
    • Example questions:
      • "What is the hardest part of managing time during preparation?"
      • "Have you ever run out of time during a speech? What happened?"
  • Write key points on a whiteboard or flip chart to identify shared experiences.

‍

3. Activity 1: Preparation Time Drill (25 minutes)

‍

Step 1: Provide the Motion ( 5 minute)

  • Announce the debate motion, e.g., “This House believes that social media does more harm than good.”

Step 2: Preparation Phase (15 minutes)

  • Instruct participants to prepare their arguments within a structured 10-minute framework:
    • Brainstorming (3 minutes): Quickly write down all ideas, arguments, and examples. No filtering at this stage.
    • Argument Prioritization (3 minutes): Select the 2–3 strongest arguments from the brainstormed list.
    • Speech Outline (4 minutes): Organize chosen arguments into a structured speech (introduction, main points, conclusion).

Step 3: Reflection and Sharing (5 minutes)

  • Participants share their time allocation strategies.
  • Discuss what worked well, what was challenging, and any differences in approaches.

‍

4. Activity 2: Speech Timing Exercise (25 minutes)

‍

Step 1: Set the Stage (5 minutes)

  • Assign a motion, e.g., “This House would ban fast fashion.”
  • Explain that participants will practice delivering a short speech with a focus on time management.

Step 2: Individual Practice (15 minutes)

  • Each participant delivers a 2-minute speech, following this structure:
    • Introduction (30 seconds): State the stance and provide a brief overview of the arguments.
    • Main Points (1 minute): Present 2–3 arguments with concise explanations.
    • Conclusion (30 seconds): Summarize key points and reinforce the stance.
  • Use a timer to ensure strict adherence to the allocated times.

Step 3: Feedback and Discussion (5 minutes)

  • Peers and the trainer provide feedback on:
    • How well time was managed within the speech.
    • The balance of time between the introduction, main points, and conclusion.

‍

5. Wrap-Up and Tips (10 minutes)

‍

  • Summarize key lessons learned during the session.
  • Share practical tips for improving time management in debates, such as:
    • Setting priorities during preparation.
    • Practicing speeches with a timer to internalize time limits.
    • Staying calm under pressure to think clearly and efficiently.
  • Encourage participants to apply these techniques in future debates.
fields.content-02:
Objectives

‍

  • Understand the importance of time management during preparation and speeches.
  • Learn techniques for allocating time effectively across argument development, rebuttals, and summaries.
  • Practice managing limited time under pressure while maintaining clarity and quality of arguments.
fields.content-03:
Learning outcomes

‍

By the end of the session, participants will:

  1. Be able to allocate preparation time effectively during debates.
  2. Develop a structured plan for managing time during speeches.
  3. Enhance their ability to prioritize tasks in high-pressure situations.
fields.content-04:
Materials and tools

‍

  • Timer or stopwatch.
  • Debate motion examples (prepared in advance).
  • Worksheets for speech outlines and time allocation.
  • Whiteboard or flip chart for group discussions.
fields.content-05:
Assessment

‍

  • Observations during activities.
  • Peer feedback on time management.
  • Self-assessment using a provided checklist.

‍

Tips and tricks

‍

  • Use a timer during practice to simulate debate conditions.
  • Start with smaller tasks and gradually increase complexity.
  • Encourage participants to create a personal strategy for time allocation.
fields.extra-text:
Appendix 1

‍

Debate Motion Examples

‍

  1. This House believes that tourism should be regulated to protect local communities and the environment.
  2. This House believes that hospitality workers deserve higher wages due to the demanding nature of their jobs.
  3. This House believes that electric cars should be prioritized in car repair and maintenance programs.
  4. This House would make financial literacy a compulsory subject in vocational schools.
  5. This House believes that sustainable practices should be mandatory in the food and beverage industry.
  6. This House believes that technology in automotive repair should be taught in all mechanic courses.
  7. This House would introduce a cap on the number of tourists allowed in a city to prevent overcrowding.
  8. This House believes that modern accounting practices should focus on environmental impact.
  9. This House would make customer service training a compulsory part of all hospitality education.
  10. This House believes that personal grooming and appearance are essential parts of a professional identity for hairdressers and beauticians.

‍

Appendix 2

‍

Worksheet for Speech Outlines and Time Allocation

Title: Speech Preparation and Time Management

Motion

Write the debate motion here

1. Time allocation — preparation phase (10 minutes)

Task Suggested time Your actual time Notes
Brainstorming 3 minutes Ideas and examples.
Argument selection 3 minutes Prioritize key arguments.
Speech outline 4 minutes Structure your speech.

2. Speech outline — speech duration: 2 minutes

Section Suggested time Main points
Introduction 30 seconds Briefly state your stance and introduce arguments.
Main points 1 minute 1. Argument 1: [Brief explanation]

‍

Appendix 3 


Reflection and Self-Assessment

‍

  • Did you use your time effectively during preparation?
    • (Yes/No, and why)
  • Did you manage to stay within the speech time limits?
    • (Yes/No, and any observations)
  • What could you improve for next time?
    • (Write your notes here)

‍
